description: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration granted approval to pembrolizumab in combination with paclitaxel, with or without bevacizumab, for the treatment of adult patients with platinum-resistant epithelial ovarian, fallopian tube, or primary peritoneal carcinoma whose tumors express PD-L1 (CPS >= 1) as determined by an FDA-authorized test, and who have received one or two prior systemic treatment regimens.

contributions.description: This indication received initial traditional approval from the U.S. FDA on 2026-02-10.

contributor.name: Food and Drug Administration
contributor.description: Regulatory agency that approves drugs for use in the United States.

indication.indication: KEYTRUDA is a programmed death receptor-1 (PD-1)-blocking antibody indicated in combination with paclitaxel, with or without bevacizumab, for the treatment of adult patients with platinum-resistant epithelial ovarian, fallopian tube, or primary peritoneal carcinoma whose tumors express PD-L1 (CPS >= 1) as determined by an FDA-authorized test, and who have recieved one or two prior systemic treatment regimens.
indication.description: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration granted approval to pembrolizumab in combination with paclitaxel, with or without bevacizumab, for the treatment of adult patients with platinum-resistant epithelial ovarian, fallopian tube, or primary peritoneal carcinoma whose tumors express PD-L1 (CPS >= 1) as determined by an FDA-authorized test, and who have received one or two prior systemic treatment regimens.
indication.raw_cancer_type: epithelial ovarian, fallopian tube, or primary peritoneal carcinoma
indication.raw_therapeutics: keytruda in combination with paclitaxel, with or without bevacizumab
